protocol.name: layer_usable_area
protocol.copyright:

description.summary: report the usable (non-exclusive) area of a surface's output

protocol.description:
This protocol lets a layer shell surface learn the usable area of the output it is shown on: the output's logical geometry minus every exclusive zone reserved by other layer surfaces (panels, docks, bars).
A centered or anchored overlay (launcher, switcher, system dialog) cannot compute this itself — only the compositor knows the exclusive zones of the other surfaces, and a non-overlapping surface receives nothing from the overlap-notify protocol. With the usable area in hand the client can center its content in the space actually free of panels rather than the full output, on the correct per-output basis and updated live as panels appear, disappear, resize, or toggle auto-hide.
Warning! The protocol described in this file is currently in the testing phase. Backward compatible changes may be added together with the corresponding interface version bump. Backward incompatible changes can only be done by creating a new major version of the extension.

interface.name: layer_usable_area_manager_v1

description.summary: usable area manager for layer surfaces

interface.description: Manager for creating usable-area feedback objects for layer shell surfaces.

request.name: destroy
request.description: Informs the server that the client will no longer use this global. Existing usable-area objects remain valid until destroyed.

request.name: get_usable_area

description.summary: get usable-area feedback for a surface

request.description:
Create a usable-area feedback object for the given wl_surface, which must be a layer shell surface.
If the given wl_surface already has a layer_usable_area_v1 object associated, the already_exists protocol error is raised.
The compositor sends an initial usable_area event as soon as the surface's output is known, and again whenever the usable area of that output changes.

entry.name: already_exists
entry.summary: surface already has a usable-area object

interface.name: layer_usable_area_v1
interface.description: Reports the usable (non-exclusive) area of the output the associated surface is currently shown on. The rectangle is expressed in the output's logical coordinate space (the same space as xdg-output logical geometry), so the location is relative to the output's top-left corner.

request.name: destroy
request.description: Informs the compositor that the client is no longer interested in usable-area updates for the surface.

event.name: usable_area
event.description: Sent by the compositor with the current usable area of the output the surface is shown on, in that output's logical coordinates. Sent once when the object is created (once the output is known) and again whenever the value changes (a panel maps, unmaps, resizes, or its exclusive zone changes, or the output is reconfigured).

entry.name: surface_destroyed
entry.summary: the associated surface has been destroyed
